title: Selectors, Cardinality, Domain Types, and Extraction Drift · Go Build Room
summary: A cooperative of small public-service publishers incrementally builds typed cardinality-aware extraction pipeline from original deterministic HTTP, robots, sitemap, HTML, graph, and report fixtures while retaining prior Go, web, Git, accessibility, security, testing, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of typed cardinality-aware extraction pipeline while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, change…
objectives: Define selection root, selector support, expected cardinality, fallback, exclusion, and source evidence for each field.
Return explicit zero, one, many, invalid, conflicting, and fallback outcomes rather than hiding them behind Find and First.
Parse external strings through constructors for URLs, language, dates, identifiers, enumerations, and bounded text.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Selector Scope
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop Go team handles case 7622 during a keyboard-first accessible report review. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove selector scope, cardinality, domain constructors, field normalization, and drift evidence; browser Go remains deterministic and free of native network, parser, filesystem, process, and host effects; then change one fuzz, race, mutation, capacity, binary, deployment, incident, restore, rollback, or residual-risk condition and reconcile release evidence. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser-service, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, x/net/html, goquery, Colly, files, processes, race, load, assistive-technology review, containers, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Define selection root, selector support, expected cardinality, fallback, exclusion, and source evidence for each field.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Authorized site auditing and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Selector Scope.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for define selection root, selector support, expected cardinality, fallback, exclusion, and source evidence for each field. For Authorized site auditing and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Selector Scope, keep Selector Scope connected to executable Go authorization, URL, robots, response, tree, frontier, goroutine, report, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.
